programs.name: Medicine (MBBS/MD)
programs.description: Five-to-six-year clinical degree training doctors, almost universally requiring Chemistry and Biology plus an admissions test and interview.
programs.grade_band: Top tier — typically A*AA/AAA (UK) or 90%+ aggregate, plus UCAT/BMAT/MCAT and interview

careers.title: Hospital Doctor / Physician
careers.note: Core clinical practice across specialties after postgraduate training.

careers.title: Surgeon
careers.note: Requires further specialist surgical training after the base degree.

careers.title: General Practitioner
careers.note: Community-based primary care after GP specialty training.

careers.title: Medical Researcher
careers.note: Clinical or lab-based research, often combined with practice.

careers.title: Public Health Physician
careers.note: Population-level health policy and epidemiology roles.

programs.outlook: Consistently high global demand; one of the most oversubscribed and competitive routes everywhere it is offered.
programs.alt_pathways: Pre-med foundation years, biomedical science as a stepping stone, or graduate-entry medicine after a first degree.

programs.name: Dentistry (BDS)
programs.description: Clinical degree training dentists, with subject demands very close to Medicine.
programs.grade_band: Top tier — typically A*AA/AAA plus UCAT/DAT and interview

careers.title: General Dentist
careers.note: Primary dental care in practice or hospital settings.

careers.title: Orthodontist
careers.note: Specialist tooth/jaw alignment practice after further training.

careers.title: Oral & Maxillofacial Surgeon
careers.note: Combined dental and medical surgical specialty.

programs.outlook: Stable, steady global demand; private practice ownership is a common long-term career path.
programs.alt_pathways: Biomedical or dental-hygiene routes as alternative entry if direct admission is not achieved.

programs.name: Veterinary Science (BVSc/DVM)
programs.description: Clinical degree training vets to treat animals, requiring strong biology and chemistry plus extensive animal-handling work experience.
programs.grade_band: Top tier — typically A*AA/AAA plus substantial documented animal/work experience
programs.outlook: Strong, steady demand; relatively few training places worldwide keeps competition high.
programs.alt_pathways: Animal science or biology degrees as a stepping stone toward graduate-entry veterinary programmes.

programs.name: Pharmacy (BPharm/PharmD)
programs.description: Degree training pharmacists in drug science, dispensing and patient care.
programs.grade_band: Competitive — typically AAA/AAB or 85%+ aggregate

careers.title: Community Pharmacist
careers.note: Retail/community pharmacy dispensing and patient advice.

programs.outlook: Stable demand worldwide; pharma R&D roles growing faster than retail pharmacy in most markets.
programs.alt_pathways: Biomedical/chemistry degrees can lead into pharma roles without the clinical pharmacist license.

programs.name: Nursing
programs.description: Clinical degree training registered nurses across adult, paediatric, mental-health or community specialisms.
programs.grade_band: Standard-to-competitive — typically BBB-AAB or 70-80%+ aggregate

careers.title: Specialist/Advanced Nurse Practitioner
careers.note: Extended clinical role after further training.

careers.title: Nurse Educator
careers.note: Training the next generation of nurses.

programs.outlook: Persistently high global demand and recognised skills shortages in many countries.
programs.alt_pathways: Healthcare assistant routes or nursing associate programmes can lead into registered nursing later.

programs.name: Computer Science / Software Engineering
programs.description: Covers software development, systems, theory of computation and (at many universities) specialisms in AI, cybersecurity and data — Mathematics is the universal gatekeeper subject.
programs.grade_band: Competitive-to-top tier — typically A*AA-AAB or 80%+ aggregate, A* in Maths at the most selective schools

careers.title: Software Engineer
careers.note: Building and maintaining applications and systems.

careers.title: Cybersecurity Analyst
careers.note: Protecting systems and data from attack.

careers.title: Game Developer
careers.note: Programming and engine work for interactive entertainment.

programs.outlook: Among the strongest-demand fields globally, especially AI, cybersecurity and cloud specialisms.
programs.alt_pathways: Bootcamps and self-taught portfolios are increasingly accepted alongside or instead of a CS degree in industry hiring.

programs.name: Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning
programs.description: A specialised computing degree focused on machine learning, statistics and intelligent systems, usually sharing a first year with Computer Science.
programs.grade_band: Top tier — typically A*AA, heavy Maths weighting

careers.title: AI Research Scientist
careers.note: Advancing the state of the art, often with a postgraduate degree.

careers.title: Data Scientist
careers.note: Applying statistical/ML methods to business problems.

programs.outlook: Fastest-growing specialism within computing; demand currently outstrips supply of qualified graduates.
programs.alt_pathways: Many AI specialists enter via a general Computer Science or Maths degree followed by a Master's.

programs.name: Cybersecurity
programs.description: Specialist computing degree focused on securing systems, networks and data against attack.
programs.grade_band: Competitive — typically AAB-ABB or 75%+ aggregate

careers.title: Security Analyst / SOC Analyst
careers.note: Monitoring and responding to security threats.

programs.outlook: Acute, well-documented global skills shortage; strong salaries and job security.
programs.alt_pathways: Industry certifications (e.g. CompTIA Security+, CEH) are widely recognised alongside or instead of a degree.

programs.name: Data Science / Business Analytics
programs.description: Blends statistics, programming and domain knowledge to extract insight from data — a strong fit for a Maths + Economics + Computer Science profile.
programs.grade_band: Competitive-to-top tier — typically A*AA with Further Maths, or A*A*A* in Maths alone without it

careers.title: Data Scientist
careers.note: Statistical modelling and machine learning on business data.

careers.title: Business Intelligence Developer
careers.note: Building data pipelines and BI tools.

careers.title: Marketing/Risk Analyst
careers.note: Applying analytics within a specific business function.

programs.outlook: One of the fastest-growing analytical career tracks globally across almost every industry.
programs.alt_pathways: Economics, Maths or CS graduates frequently move into data science via short courses or on-the-job training.

programs.name: Electrical & Electronic Engineering
programs.description: Covers power systems, electronics, telecommunications and embedded systems — Physics and Maths are the standard gatekeeper combination.
programs.grade_band: Competitive-to-top tier — typically A*AA-AAA

careers.title: Electrical Engineer
careers.note: Power systems, grids and electrical infrastructure.

programs.outlook: Steady demand, with strong growth in renewable energy and electrification of transport.
programs.alt_pathways: Technician/apprenticeship routes can lead to chartered engineer status via further study.

programs.name: Mechanical Engineering
programs.description: Covers mechanics, thermodynamics, materials and design — broadly applicable across automotive, aerospace and manufacturing.
programs.grade_band: Competitive — typically AAA-ABB

careers.title: Mechanical Engineer
careers.note: Design and analysis of mechanical systems.

programs.outlook: Consistently strong demand; a foundational engineering discipline across almost every industry.
programs.alt_pathways: Apprenticeship and technician routes through professional engineering institutions.

programs.name: Civil Engineering
programs.description: Covers structures, infrastructure, transport and construction — pairs well with Maths, Physics and Built Environment.
programs.grade_band: Competitive — typically AAA-BBB

careers.title: Water/Environmental Engineer
careers.note: Water supply, drainage and flood systems.

careers.title: Construction Project Manager
careers.note: Delivering infrastructure projects on time and budget.

programs.outlook: Steady, infrastructure-driven demand, particularly strong in rapidly urbanising countries.
programs.alt_pathways: Quantity surveying or construction-management degrees as related, slightly less technical alternatives.

programs.name: Architecture
programs.description: Design-led professional degree combining creative, technical and regulatory training; usually a 5-7 year qualifying route.
programs.grade_band: Competitive — typically AAB-ABB plus a creative portfolio

careers.title: Architect
careers.note: Building design, after completing the full professional qualifying route.

programs.outlook: Cyclical with construction markets, but a stable long-term professional career path.
programs.alt_pathways: Architectural technology degrees offer a shorter, more technical alternative route.

programs.name: Economics
programs.description: Analyses how individuals, firms, and governments allocate resources — a strong, direct continuation of an Economics A-Level.
programs.grade_band: Competitive-to-top tier — typically A*AA-AAB or 80%+ aggregate

careers.title: Economist
careers.note: Government, central bank or think-tank economic analysis.

careers.title: Investment Banker / Analyst
careers.note: Financial markets and corporate finance roles.

programs.outlook: Strong, stable demand; a widely respected, flexible degree for finance, policy and business careers alike.
programs.alt_pathways: Joint degrees (Economics & Maths, Economics & CS) widen options without needing a separate subject.

programs.name: Business & Management
programs.description: Broad business foundation covering strategy, operations, marketing and organisational behaviour.
programs.grade_band: Standard-to-competitive — typically ABB-BBB or 70%+ aggregate
programs.outlook: Broad, stable demand across virtually every industry worldwide.
programs.alt_pathways: Specialised variants (marketing, HR, supply chain) are common alternative entry points.

programs.name: Accounting & Finance
programs.description: Covers financial reporting, corporate finance and investment analysis — a direct continuation of an Accounting A-Level.
programs.grade_band: Competitive — typically A*AA-AAB or 75%+ aggregate

careers.title: Chartered Accountant
careers.note: Audit, tax or financial reporting after professional qualification (ACA/ACCA/CA).

careers.title: Investment Banker
careers.note: Capital markets and corporate finance deals.

careers.title: Corporate Treasurer
careers.note: Managing a company's cash, risk and funding.

programs.outlook: Consistently strong, globally portable demand; professional qualification (ACCA/ACA/CPA) significantly boosts prospects.
programs.alt_pathways: Professional accounting qualifications (ACCA, CIMA) can be studied alongside or instead of a full degree.

programs.name: Actuarial Science
programs.description: Applies maths, statistics and economics to assess financial risk, especially in insurance and pensions.
programs.grade_band: Top tier — typically A*AA-AAA, near-universal requirement for an A in Maths

careers.title: Actuary
careers.note: Pricing and risk assessment for insurance/pensions, via professional exams post-degree.

careers.title: Risk Analyst
careers.note: Quantitative risk management in banking or insurance.

careers.title: Pensions Consultant
careers.note: Advising on pension scheme design and funding.

careers.title: Catastrophe Modeller
careers.note: Quantifying risk from natural disasters for (re)insurers.

programs.outlook: Consistently ranked among the highest-paying, most stable quantitative careers globally.
programs.alt_pathways: Maths or Statistics graduates can qualify as actuaries by passing professional body exams (IFoA, SOA) after a related degree.

programs.name: Mathematics & Statistics
programs.description: Pure and applied mathematics, statistics and (at many universities) financial or computational mathematics.
programs.grade_band: Top tier at elite departments (Further Maths often the stated minimum, e.g. Cambridge); A*AA-AAB elsewhere

careers.title: Quantitative Analyst ("Quant")
careers.note: Mathematical modelling in finance and trading.

careers.title: Data Scientist
careers.note: Statistical and machine-learning roles across industry.

programs.outlook: Extremely flexible degree; consistently strong demand across finance, tech and research.
programs.alt_pathways: Joint Maths degrees (Maths & Economics, Maths & CS) broaden career options further.

programs.name: Law (LLB/JD)
programs.description: Foundational legal training; in the UK/Pakistan an undergraduate LLB, in the US a postgraduate JD after any first degree.
programs.grade_band: Competitive-to-top tier — typically A*AA-AAB or 80%+ aggregate

careers.title: Solicitor / Advocate
careers.note: Client-facing legal practice after further professional training.

careers.title: Judge
careers.note: Senior judiciary role, typically after years of practice.

programs.outlook: Stable, prestige-linked demand; corporate and commercial law specialisms currently strongest.
programs.alt_pathways: Graduate-entry law (GDL/JD-style) lets non-law graduates qualify after a different first degree.

programs.name: Psychology
programs.description: Scientific study of mind and behaviour; a flexible degree feeding into clinical, organisational and research careers.
programs.grade_band: Competitive — typically AAB-ABB or 75%+ aggregate

careers.title: UX Researcher
careers.note: User-behaviour research in tech product teams.

programs.outlook: Growing demand, especially in mental-health services and UX/behavioural-research roles in tech.
programs.alt_pathways: Many psychology graduates move into HR, marketing or UX without further clinical training.

programs.name: Natural Sciences (Biology / Chemistry / Physics)
programs.description: Single or combined physical/life-science degrees feeding into research, industry and further professional study.
programs.grade_band: Competitive — typically AAA-ABB depending on the specific science

careers.title: Research Scientist
careers.note: Academic or industrial R&D, often requiring a postgraduate degree.

careers.title: Lab/Quality Analyst
careers.note: Applied testing and quality-control roles in industry.

careers.title: Science Communicator/Writer
careers.note: Translating science for media and the public.

programs.outlook: Stable; R&D-heavy industries (pharma, biotech, materials) show the strongest long-term growth.
programs.alt_pathways: A general science degree keeps medicine, engineering and teaching routes open via further study.

programs.name: Media, Journalism & Communication
programs.description: Covers reporting, broadcasting, PR and digital content — a natural fit for English, Urdu, Media Studies or Journalism A-Levels.
programs.grade_band: Standard-to-competitive — typically ABB-BBB or 65-75% aggregate

careers.title: Journalist / Reporter
careers.note: Print, broadcast or digital news reporting.

careers.title: Content Creator / Strategist
careers.note: Digital content for brands and platforms.

programs.outlook: Traditional journalism roles are shrinking, but digital content, PR and communications roles are growing.
programs.alt_pathways: Portfolio-building (blogging, podcasting, video) is increasingly as valued by employers as the degree itself.

programs.name: Languages, Linguistics & Area Studies
programs.description: Covers modern/classical languages, linguistics and regional studies (e.g. South Asian Studies) — directly extends a language A-Level such as Urdu, Arabic or French.
programs.grade_band: Standard-to-competitive — typically ABB-AAB or 65-75% aggregate
programs.outlook: Steady niche demand; rare-language and technical-translation skills command a premium.
programs.alt_pathways: Joint honours (Language + Business/Law/Politics) broaden career options significantly.

programs.name: Social Sciences (Politics, Sociology, International Relations)
programs.description: Covers how societies, states and institutions work — a strong fit for History, Politics, Sociology or Global Perspectives A-Levels.
programs.grade_band: Standard-to-competitive — typically ABB-AAB or 70%+ aggregate

careers.title: Political Researcher / Journalist
careers.note: Research for politicians, think tanks or media.

programs.outlook: Stable demand in public policy and international development; highly transferable analytical skill set.
programs.alt_pathways: Often combined with a language or economics as a joint honours degree.
